hops.context: the NanoXplore synthesis tool has been integrated with Bambu.
its back-end has been customized to support the NG-ULTRA FPGA.
its back-end has been customized to support the NG-ULTRA FPGA.

question: Considering the integration and customization of the NanoXplore synthesis tool with Bambu, what is the primary type of device that NG-ULTRA, as configured in the back-end of Bambu, represents?

options.A: NG-ULTRA is primarily designed as a general-purpose microprocessor.
options.B: NG-ULTRA is primarily designed as a digital signal processor.
options.C: NG-ULTRA is primarily designed as a field-programmable gate array.
options.D: NG-ULTRA is primarily designed as an application-specific integrated circuit.

explanation: The knowledge path from the NanoXplore synthesis tool (NXmap) to Bambu, and further to NG-ULTRA, ends by identifying NG-ULTRA as a type of FPGA (Field-Programmable Gate Array). This indicates that NG-ULTRA, which is customized in the back-end of Bambu, is specifically a FPGA rather than any other type of device.
